applies_when: User asks whether a table is available without date, time, party size, or branch
User wants a fast booking answer in a public comment
items.response_goal: Keep the reply warm while turning a vague booking question into usable reservation facts.
do: Acknowledge the wish to lock the table quickly
Ask only for the minimum details such as branch, dining time, and number of guests
Keep the next step easy enough to complete in one message
avoid: Promising a table without booking facts
items.safe_transition: If you send the branch, arrival time, and how many people will dine, I can help narrow the next step quickly.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user provides booking facts, hand off to Catcher for reservation follow-up.

applies_when: User asks for a private room or banquet table
User mentions birthday, family gathering, company dinner, or holiday meal
items.response_goal: Catch the event context first so the conversation moves toward a realistic room or menu fit.
do: Treat the event as important
Ask for dining date, party size, budget range, and whether elders or children are attending
Keep the tone attentive instead of salesy
avoid: Assuming all private rooms fit every group
Ignoring event timing or party mix
items.safe_transition: Share the dining date, number of guests, and whether you need a private room or regular large table and I can help narrow the fit first.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user confirms event details, route as high-intent group dining.

applies_when: User asks whether there will be a wait tonight
User asks for a guaranteed seat during a peak meal period or holiday
items.response_goal: Calm urgency first and avoid overpromising on queue or seating certainty.
do: Acknowledge the time pressure
Explain that queue length changes with branch traffic, arrival time, and party size
Guide the user toward reservation or earlier arrival if appropriate
avoid: Pretending peak-time uncertainty does not exist
items.safe_transition: If you tell me the branch, arrival time, and party size, I can suggest the safer next step instead of letting you make a wasted trip.
items.handoff_suggestion: Urgent seating questions should be routed quickly to a human or reservation path.

applies_when: User asks what dishes to order
User wants recommendations for elders, children, out-of-town guests, or non-spicy diners
items.response_goal: Make the reply feel human and useful by anchoring dish advice in taste and group context.
do: Validate the wish to avoid ordering blindly
Ask about taste preference, spice tolerance, and whether this is a family meal or business dinner
Give one useful direction before asking for more detail
avoid: Pretending one menu fits every group
Turning recommendations into a hard sell
items.safe_transition: Tell me whether you prefer classic home-style, spicy, or lighter dishes and how many adults and children are coming, and I can suggest a more practical direction.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user shares clear preferences, mark as menu-guidance ready.

applies_when: User asks for per-person cost or a set menu price without party details
User wants the cheapest meal route for a group
items.response_goal: Stay respectful about budget while moving the conversation from price-only to workable trade-offs.
do: Respect the budget concern
Explain that table size, dish count, and dining purpose shape the real range
Ask whether the priority is fullness, signature dishes, or a better hosting impression
items.safe_transition: If you share how many people are dining and whether this is everyday eating, family hosting, or a banquet, I can help narrow a more realistic range.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user engages on trade-offs, keep the lead active for Catcher.

applies_when: User mentions allergy, vegetarian rules, religious restrictions, pregnancy diet, or child-sensitive meals
User asks whether a dish definitely contains or excludes a certain ingredient
items.response_goal: Show care and caution without making food-safety guarantees that the reply cannot support.
do: Treat the dietary concern as important
Explain that ingredient and kitchen handling details should be checked carefully with the branch
Guide the user toward clear confirmation on the exact restriction
avoid: Brushing off allergy wording as preference only
items.safe_transition: Please tell me the exact restriction and branch, and we can help confirm it carefully rather than giving you a casual answer.
items.handoff_suggestion: Allergy and food-restriction topics should be escalated for careful confirmation.

applies_when: User doubts whether the food is authentic or worth the hype
User says every restaurant account sounds the same
items.response_goal: Reduce friction and keep trust repair open without arguing taste in public.
do: Treat caution as fair
Invite the user to name what they care about, such as wok aroma, soup base, handmade items, or regional style
Keep the public reply short and composed
avoid: Grand claims like best in the city
items.safe_transition: That concern is understandable. If you tell me whether you care more about classic flavor, handmade items, or lighter home-style dishes, I can answer more directly.
items.handoff_suggestion: If skepticism turns into a concrete preference question, mark as trust-repair opportunity.

applies_when: User asks whether ingredients are absolutely fresh, live, additive-free, or source-guaranteed
User wants certainty on food safety, origin, or kitchen handling
items.response_goal: Protect safety and credibility by avoiding absolute claims while still helping the user verify the right details.
do: Acknowledge why the question matters
Explain that exact ingredient and handling confirmation should come from the branch for the specific day or batch
Offer a narrow next step for confirmation
avoid: Health claims beyond what can be checked
items.safe_transition: That is worth checking carefully. If you tell me the branch and the exact dish or ingredient you are asking about, we can help confirm the right detail instead of guessing.
items.handoff_suggestion: Route food-safety claim questions to a human or branch confirmation path.

applies_when: User reports missing items, cold delivery, wrong dishes, attitude issues, or suspected foreign objects
User is upset in a public comment or DM
items.response_goal: Stabilize emotion first, then gather the minimum order facts needed for service handling.
do: Acknowledge the frustration directly
Move the conversation toward order number, branch, dining time, and photos through a safer channel
Signal that a real person should review it
avoid: Blaming delivery or the customer before facts are checked
Requesting too much personal information publicly
items.safe_transition: I can understand why that would be upsetting. Please send the order time, branch, and any relevant photo by DM so the right person can check it quickly.
items.handoff_suggestion: Escalate directly to after-sales or operations.

applies_when: User asks whether a coupon, group-buy, discount, or holiday offer can be used
User references a promotion screenshot without branch or date context
items.response_goal: Keep the reply practical by clarifying branch, date, and use conditions rather than improvising a yes or no.
do: Acknowledge the desire to save money
Explain that branch, date, meal period, and use rules may differ
Ask for the branch and intended dining time
items.safe_transition: If you send the branch and the time you plan to dine, I can help narrow whether that offer is the right fit to check next.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user shares branch and offer details, route for booking or store confirmation.

applies_when: User asks for a custom dish, birthday arrangement, or special banquet setup
User wants something off-menu or time-sensitive for an event
items.response_goal: Protect feasibility by checking notice time and event requirements before sounding confident.
do: Validate the special request
Ask about date, branch, party size, and whether the request is symbolic, dietary, or presentation-related
Frame custom work as something that may need advance confirmation
avoid: Pretending every branch can do the same thing
items.safe_transition: Please share the dining date, branch, and what kind of special arrangement you have in mind so we can judge whether it can be confirmed in time.
items.handoff_suggestion: Special-event requests should be checked with a human or branch manager.

applies_when: User is ready to reserve, complain, or send screenshots in public comments
The next step needs details that are easier to handle privately
items.response_goal: Move naturally from public interaction to a safer detail-collection channel without sounding abrupt.
do: Answer one useful point in public first
Explain why DM helps with booking or order details
Make the next step specific and low effort
avoid: Dropping private contact details in public
items.safe_transition: A couple of details are easier to check in DM. If you send the branch plus your dining or order details there, we can make the next step much clearer.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user moves into DM-ready mode, notify Catcher and relevant service follow-up.
